name: Orange Beef
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Boneless top sirloin steak, partially frozen
2 tablespoons Finely chopped fresh orange -or tangerine peel
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the partially frozen steak across the grain into paper-thin slices about 1 1/2 inches long and 1 inch wide.
2. In a medium bowl, combine 1 tablespoon soy sauce and 1 teaspoon sugar. Add the beef slices and toss to coat. Mix in the egg white, then add 1 tablespoon of cornstarch, mixing with fingers to distribute evenly.
3. Heat oil over high heat in a wok or 10-inch skillet until hot. Add the beef and stir-fry until the meat just loses its redness, about 3 minutes. Remove with a slotted spoon to a dish.
4. In the remaining oil in the wok, stir-fry the orange peel over medium heat for 2 to 3 minutes to soften. Mix in the orange juice concentrate, the remaining 3 tablespoons soy sauce, the remaining 1/3 cup sugar, the ketchup, and 1/4 cup water. Add the orange extract. Heat to boiling.
5. Dissolve the remaining 1 tablespoon cornstarch in the remaining 1 tablespoon water. Add to the wok. Cook, stirring constantly, until the sauce boils and thickens, 1 to 2 minutes.
6. Return the beef to the wok and heat through, 1 to 2 minutes. Serve over hot cooked rice, garnished with parsley and orange slices if desired.
